description: Accesses values from the data layer (window.dataLayer or window._mtm) for dynamic content and tracking. Essential for retrieving ecommerce data, user information, and custom values pushed to the data layer by your website or application.

when_to_use: Access ecommerce data (transaction details, product information, cart contents)
Retrieve user information (user ID, customer tier, login status)
Get dynamic page data (category, product ID, search terms)
Access custom events and values pushed by your application
Integrate with GTM data layer for seamless migration
Create conditional logic based on data layer values
best_practices: Use consistent naming conventions across your data layer
Test data layer variables in browser console before deployment
Validate that the required values are pushed for every relevant user journey
Use descriptive variable names that indicate the data source
Consider data layer availability timing - some values may load asynchronously
Document your data layer structure for team collaboration

create_variable.description: Create a new data layer variable to access values from window.dataLayer or window._mtm

variable_type.description: Must be 'data_layer' for data layer variables

value.description: Path to the property to read from the data layer
Path to the property to access in the data layer
value.recommendation: Use dot notation for nested objects (e.g., 'ecommerce.purchase.value') and bracket notation for arrays (e.g., 'products[0].name'). Confirm the same structure exists in your data layer implementation.

create_example.description: Creating a new data layer variable for ecommerce order total

update_example.description: Updating an existing data layer variable to change the data source

array_access_example.description: Accessing array elements in data layer

common_mistakes: Using incorrect property names or paths for nested data
Leaving the data layer path empty or pointing to a property that is not pushed on every page
Assuming data layer is always available immediately on page load
Not testing data layer variables in different scenarios (logged in/out, different pages)
Using spaces or special characters in the value path
Not documenting the expected data layer structure for the team

variable_returns_undefined.problem: Data layer variable returns undefined or empty values
solutions: Check that the data layer property name exactly matches your website's implementation
Verify the data layer is populated before the variable is accessed
Test the data layer structure in browser console: window.dataLayer or window._mtm
Ensure proper timing - some data may load asynchronously
Check for typos in nested property paths

nested_properties_not_working.problem: Cannot access nested data layer properties
solutions: Use dot notation for nested objects: 'ecommerce.purchase.value'
Verify the nested structure exists in your data layer
Test the full path in browser console before using
